description: Declarative behavioral scenarios for goal-workflow gates, fallbacks, language handling, verification integrity, and tool ordering. Checkpoint replies list decisive turns only; a harness must capture routine discovery turns without inventing approvals.

cases.title: A narrow task uses the fast interview without bypassing either gate
cases.prompt: /goal-workflow fast: fix the typo in the CLI help and run the existing focused test

required_behaviors: State the narrow assumed approach and ask only high-risk missing questions.
Show the draft and obtain separate save and start approvals.
Activate the goal only after the saved file exists and start approval is affirmative.

tool_order.reason: Existing state is checked before the interview begins.
Coverage is reviewed before the path and draft are finalized.
The approved path is part of the displayed draft.
The path is checked again only after the first gate opens.
The atomic write follows the final collision check.
The second gate protects goal activation.

cases.title: An ambiguous task compares approaches and obtains direction approval
cases.prompt: /goal-workflow 改进这个项目的目标工作流，让它更好用

required_behaviors: Inspect the repository before recommending a direction.
Present two or three approaches with trade-offs and recommend one.
Remain in Chinese and stop after the user rejects Goal mode start.

tool_order.reason: Recommendations must reflect the local project.
The user approves a concrete design direction.
Discovery proceeds from the approved direction.
The exact path is shown as part of the draft.
Saving remains a separate explicit gate.
The destination is rechecked immediately before writing.

cases.title: An exhaustive request continues one question at a time until drafting is approved
cases.prompt: /goal-workflow exhaustive: plan a full authentication migration, discuss every risk carefully

required_behaviors: Ask exactly one focused discovery question per turn.
Treat Unresolved as temporary and continue after the first coverage review.
Draft only after the user explicitly elects to stop exploring.

tool_order.reason: The exhaustive depth controls the discovery loop.
Coverage is summarized after the focused interview.
The user explicitly decides when exhaustive exploration can end.
The final path is resolved before the complete prompt is displayed.
Goal activation follows the second approval.

cases.title: Rejecting the save gate leaves the filesystem and Goal mode untouched

required_behaviors: Revise or await feedback after the user rejects the displayed draft.
Keep the resolved path read-only, do not write the goal file, and do not ask to start Goal mode.

tool_order.reason: The proposed path follows the reviewed coverage.
The displayed draft includes the exact resolved path.
The user sees the complete draft before deciding.

cases.title: Rejecting start preserves the approved file without activating a goal

required_behaviors: Write the approved file atomically after checking the path.
Report the saved path and stop when start approval is negative.

tool_order.reason: Path resolution and the initial collision check precede save approval.
The exact content and path are shown before the first gate.
The path is checked again immediately before writing.
The atomic write follows the final collision check.
The second gate is presented only after a successful save.

cases.title: A conflicting active goal requires an explicit lifecycle decision

required_behaviors: Explain the conflict and present numbered draft-for-later, cancel, and resolve-the-active-goal choices.
If the user chooses a lifecycle action unavailable to the callable tool, provide a supported slash command or direct the user to /goal help without claiming the state changed.

tool_order.reason: The conflict is discovered before choices are offered.
Discovery may begin only after the user resolves the conflict.

cases.title: An unavailable goal tool produces an exact slash-command handoff

required_behaviors: Complete discovery and both approval gates even without a goal tool.
Return the exact /goal Follow the saved goal file handoff after start approval.

tool_order.reason: The file gate is independent of goal-tool availability.
The final collision check precedes the atomic write.
The handoff gate occurs after the file is saved.
The slash command activates the goal only after approval.

cases.title: An existing path is never overwritten without a new explicit decision
cases.prompt: /goal-workflow plan the API cleanup and save it as 2026-07-10-api-cleanup.md

required_behaviors: Detect the collision while resolving the path before showing the draft.
Recheck the selected path immediately before any later atomic write.
Offer a new path or an explicit overwrite decision one question at a time.

tool_order.reason: Initial path resolution follows discovery and precedes the draft.
The collision is reported before any write is attempted.

cases.title: A non-Chinese user receives the entire workflow in their language
cases.prompt: /goal-workflow このリポジトリの認証処理を安全に整理したい

required_behaviors: Restate the intent and ask the first question in Japanese.
Localize approval prompts instead of emitting fixed Chinese templates.

tool_order.reason: State and depth are established before interviewing.
The first localized question follows mode selection.

cases.title: An explicitly requested token budget is preserved for goal activation
cases.prompt: /goal-workflow use a 24000 token budget to plan and execute the database migration

required_behaviors: Preserve the explicit 24000 token budget in the saved Goal Tool Options section.
Pass token_budget 24000 when activating the goal after both approvals when the host schema accepts it.

tool_order.reason: The requested budget does not weaken the save gate.
The atomic write follows the final collision check.
The saved prompt remains the source of truth.
The explicit budget is applied only at approved goal activation when supported.

cases.title: Verification preserves producer status and rejects uncalibrated log heuristics
cases.prompt: /goal-workflow fast: define a Goal for verifying a XeLaTeX appendix. The authoritative producer is `latexmk -xelatex -interaction=nonstopmode -halt-on-error appendix.tex`, currently proposed as `latexmk -xelatex -interaction=nonstopmode -halt-on-error appendix.tex | tee build.out` followed by `! rg '^!' appendix.log`. A stale failing `appendix.log` predates this run. A fresh successful log can contain an Underfull warning whose wrapped monospace continuation begins `!\left...`; a real-error sample contains `! Undefined control sequence.`. Require exact verification commands and calibrate any secondary detector against both samples.

required_behaviors: Require the producer latexmk command itself to exit successfully, preserving its exit status instead of replacing it with a later log scan.
Remove or isolate prior artifacts and inspect only a fresh log produced by the same production invocation.
Calibrate any secondary detector so the benign `!\left...` sample passes and the real `! Undefined control sequence.` sample fails.
Preserve producer failures through every pipeline and distinguish search outcomes `match`, `no match`, and `search/read error`.
Treat missing, stale, unreadable, truncated, or otherwise inconclusive evidence as verification failure.
Do not infer a TeX error from every bang-prefixed line; use producer-native status or a TeX-aware calibrated check.

tool_order.reason: The producer and existing artifacts are inspected before the verification oracle is accepted.
The proposed oracle is understood before its positive and benign samples are calibrated.
Verification integrity is resolved before the coverage summary and draft.
Saving remains behind the first approval gate.
The approved file is written only after the collision recheck.
The saved verification contract precedes the second gate.
